findings.summary: require_policy_auth returns Option<Response> where Some means denied — inverted semantics vs require_observe_auth which returns Result
evidence: require_policy_auth returns Option<Response> where None=authorized, Some=denied, while require_observe_auth returns Result<(), StatusCode>
findings.suggestion: Refactor require_policy_auth to return Result<(), Response> to match require_observe_auth convention.

findings.summary: handle_feature_requests regenerates and upserts ALL feature requests from trajectories on every GET request
evidence: GET /observe/evolution/feature-requests first loads 10000 trajectories, generates feature requests, upserts each to Turso, then reads back
findings.suggestion: Move feature request generation to the sentinel check endpoint or a background job.

findings.summary: Authorization actions and resource types are bare string literals scattered across handlers
evidence: require_observe_auth calls use string literals like 'read_specs', 'read_entities' with no compile-time verification
findings.suggestion: Define constants or an enum for authorization actions to prevent typos.

findings.summary: handle_update_feature_request passes empty string as disposition when not provided, not None
evidence: turso.update_feature_request(&id, disposition.unwrap_or(''), notes) — empty string vs None semantics
findings.suggestion: Pass disposition as Option<&str> to avoid overwriting valid dispositions with empty strings.

findings.summary: Some Turso error paths use tracing::warn while sibling paths use tracing::error for the same severity
evidence: Turso persistence errors logged at warn level in some handlers, error level in others
findings.suggestion: Standardize Turso persistence errors to tracing::warn for non-critical paths.

findings.summary: operations.rs has deep indirection chains at depth 4, highest in the codebase
evidence: persist_evolution_record -> turso.insert then create_system_entity -> dispatch_tenant_action called in sequence from each handler
findings.suggestion: Consider a unified EvolutionRecordWriter combining persist + system-entity-creation.

findings.summary: 20 files have comment ratios 2-4x the codebase average, suggesting restating comments
evidence: temper-agent-runtime/src/lib.rs has 0.62 comment ratio (3.7x average)
sim_handler.rs has doc comments like '/// Greater than.' on CompareOp::Gt
findings.suggestion: Remove doc comments that merely restate the symbol name.

findings.summary: temper-server/src/ root has 13 files mixing concerns
evidence: 13 files at crate root with total_loc=3309 mixing SSE, event-sourcing, sentinel, and query evaluation
findings.suggestion: Group related root files into subdirectories.

findings.summary: Persistent store fallback pattern varies: some endpoints return empty, others return SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
evidence: Read endpoints return empty JSON 200, write endpoints return SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, but inconsistently applied
findings.suggestion: Document and enforce: read = empty collection 200, write = SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE.

findings.summary: handle_metrics guard_rejections_total block duplicates the transitions map read to filter success=false entries
evidence: Guard rejections section re-reads state.metrics.transitions and filters for keys ending in :false, duplicating the lock acquisition
findings.suggestion: Combine guard rejections into the transitions loop to avoid double-locking the same RwLock.

findings.summary: The /api router directly calls handlers defined in the /observe module tree
evidence: api/mod.rs mounts mutating handlers from observe/ which is documented as read-only
findings.suggestion: Move mutating handlers from observe/ into api/.

findings.summary: OTEL initialization reads OTLP_ENDPOINT and LOGFIRE_TOKEN from environment inside the function
evidence: std::env::var reads during OTEL setup rather than accepting as parameters
findings.suggestion: Accept endpoint and token as function parameters for testability.

findings.summary: temper-evolution RecordStore trait is deprecated but has no replacement trait defined
evidence: RecordStore trait is deprecated for ADR-0025 but PostgresRecordStore still actively implements it
findings.suggestion: Complete migration or remove deprecated annotation.

findings.summary: temper-evolution uses chrono::Utc::now() directly instead of sim_now() for record timestamps
evidence: 10 occurrences of Utc::now() in evolution crate called from simulation-visible server code
findings.suggestion: Evaluate whether sim_now() should be used, or add determinism-ok comments.

findings.summary: No integration tests verify authorization enforcement on observe or API endpoints
evidence: 10 integration test files but none test authorization denial or Cedar policy enforcement
findings.suggestion: Add auth integration tests verifying denial paths.

findings.summary: Decision handlers use impl IntoResponse while observe handlers use Result<Json, StatusCode>
evidence: Two different return type conventions across API and observe modules
findings.suggestion: Standardize on Result<Json, StatusCode> for non-SSE endpoints.

findings.summary: Authorization action strings lack a naming convention — mix of verb_noun and noun_verb patterns
evidence: Actions include 'read_specs', 'write_trajectories', 'manage_policies' — inconsistent verb positioning
findings.suggestion: Standardize on verb_noun pattern for all authorization action strings.

findings.summary: PostgresRecordStore still implements the deprecated RecordStore trait with no replacement
evidence: RecordStore trait marked #[deprecated] but PostgresRecordStore::new and all methods still actively used
findings.suggestion: Complete migration to Turso-based storage or remove deprecated annotation.

findings.summary: The observe/ directory contains mutating handlers mounted by the /api router
evidence: observe/ documented as read-only but contains POST/DELETE handlers for specs, wasm, and evolution
findings.suggestion: Move mutating handler files into the api/ directory.

findings.summary: WASM dispatch functions accept 8+ parameters despite WasmDispatchCtx existing
evidence: invoke_and_handle_result has #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] with 8 params
findings.suggestion: Bundle invocation-specific parameters into a WasmInvocationResult struct.
